body:
## Summary

Fixes #682. The OpenAI Chat Completions request parser rejected an assistant
message carrying an explicit `"content": null` together with `tool_calls`,
returning:

```
HTTP 400 invalid chat request: data did not match any variant of untagged enum MessageContent
```

`content: null` on an assistant tool-call turn is the standard OpenAI shape and
is exactly what **Qwen Code (`qwen`)** emits, so the qwen agent loop died mid-run
as soon as it had to replay a prior assistant tool-call turn.

## Root cause

`ChatMessage.content` is typed `MessageContent`, an `#[serde(untagged)]` enum
(`Text(String) | Parts(Vec<..>)`, no unit variant) with `#[serde(default)]`.
`#[serde(default)]` only supplies the default when the key is **absent**; an
explicit `null` is still fed to the untagged enum, which has no variant that
matches `null`, so deserialization fails. Omitting `content` or sending
`content: ""` both already returned 200 — only the explicit JSON `null` failed.

## Fix

Map an explicit `null` to the default via a small deserializer in
`src/protocol.rs`:

```rust
#[serde(default, deserialize_with = "deserialize_null_content")]
pub content: MessageContent,

fn deserialize_null_content<'de, D>(deserializer: D) -> Result<MessageContent, D::Error>
where
    D: serde::Deserializer<'de>,
{
    Ok(Option::<MessageContent>::deserialize(deserializer)?.unwrap_or_default())
}
```

## Reproduction

```bash
curl -sS -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}\n" \
  http://127.0.0.1:8080/api/openai/v1/chat/completions \
  -H 'content-type: application/json' -H 'authorization: Bearer formal-ai' \
  -d '{"model":"formal-ai","messages":[
        {"role":"assistant","content":null,
         "tool_calls":[{"id":"c1","type":"function",
           "function":{"name":"grep_search","arguments":"{\"query\":\"x\"}"}}]}]}'
# before: 400   after: 200
```

## Tests

Added to `tests/unit/specification/openai_compatibility.rs`:

- `chat_completion_accepts_explicit_null_content_on_tool_call_turns` — parses the
  exact null-content tool-call shape and asserts `null`, `""`, and an omitted
  `content` key all deserialize to the same default empty content.
- `chat_completion_endpoint_accepts_explicit_null_content` — server-level
  reproduction of the reporter's minimal request; asserts it now returns 200.

Verified the server-level test **fails without the fix** (400) and **passes with
it** (200). Full `cargo test --test unit` suite is green (1549 passed);
`cargo fmt --check` and `cargo clippy --all-targets` are clean.

A `changelog.d` fragment (`bump: patch`) is included.

commits.messageBody:
Adding .gitkeep for PR creation (default mode).
This file will be removed when the task is complete.

Issue: https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/issues/682

Gather issue, PR, related-issue, and source-level root-cause data for
the explicit "content": null tool-call 400 defect into a structured
log folder (raw gh captures + analysis markdown).
commits.messageHeadline: docs: compile issue #682 data into dev/log/issues/682/pulls/685
Add development log for issue #682 PR #685

commits.messageBody:
…urns (#682)

OpenAI Chat Completions rejected an assistant message carrying an explicit
"content": null together with tool_calls, returning 400 "data did not match
any variant of untagged enum MessageContent". content: null on a tool-call
turn is the standard OpenAI shape and is what Qwen Code emits, so the qwen
agent loop died as soon as it replayed a prior assistant tool-call turn.

ChatMessage.content is an untagged enum (Text | Parts) with #[serde(default)],
which only supplies the default when the key is absent; an explicit null was
still fed to the enum, which has no unit variant. Add a small deserializer
that maps an explicit null to the default empty content.

Adds regression tests: request-level parse of the exact null-content shape
and a server-level reproduction of the reporter's minimal request now
returning 200.
commits.messageHeadline: fix(protocol): accept explicit content: null on assistant tool-call t…

commits.messageBody:
The two new regression tests pushed openai_compatibility.rs to 1060 lines,
over the 1000-line lint limit. Move them into tests/unit/specification/issue_682.rs
(matching the existing issue_NNN.rs convention) so the source file stays under
its cap.
commits.messageHeadline: test: move #682 regression tests to dedicated issue_682 module

title: fix: accept explicit content: null on assistant tool-call turns (#682, breaks qwen)
